요한복음 6:1.content: After these things, Jesus went away to the other side of the sea of Galilee, which is also called the Sea of Tiberias.

요한복음 6:2.content: A great multitude followed him, because they saw his signs which he did on those who were sick.

요한복음 6:3.content: Jesus went up into the mountain, and he sat there with his disciples.

요한복음 6:4.content: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was at hand.

요한복음 6:5.content: Jesus therefore lifting up his eyes, and seeing that a great multitude was coming to him, said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread, that these may eat?”

요한복음 6:6.content: He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he would do.

요한복음 6:7.content: Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may receive a little.”

요한복음 6:8.content: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,

요한복음 6:9.content: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these among so many?”

요한복음 6:10.content: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” Now there was much grass in that place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.

요한복음 6:11.content: Jesus took the loaves; and having given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to those who were sitting down; likewise also of the fish as much as they desired.

요한복음 6:12.content: When they were filled, he said to his disciples, “Gather up the broken pieces which are left over, that nothing be lost.”

요한복음 6:13.content: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with broken pieces from the five barley loaves, which were left over by those who had eaten.

요한복음 6:14.content: When therefore the people saw the sign which Jesus did, they said, “This is truly the prophet who comes into the world.”

요한복음 6:15.content: Jesus therefore, perceiving that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, withdrew again to the mountain by himself.

요한복음 6:16.content: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea.

요한복음 6:17.content: They entered into the boat, and were going over the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not come to them.

요한복음 6:18.content: The sea was tossed by a great wind blowing.

요한복음 6:19.content: When therefore they had rowed about twenty-five or thirty stadia, they saw Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing near to the boat; and they were afraid.

요한복음 6:20.content: But he said to them, “It is I. Don’t be afraid.”

요한복음 6:21.content: They were willing therefore to receive him into the boat. Immediately the boat was at the land where they were going.

요한복음 6:22.content: On the next day, the multitude that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other boat there, except the one in which his disciples had embarked, and that Jesus hadn’t entered with his disciples into the boat, but his disciples had gone away alone.

요한복음 6:23.content: However boats from Tiberias came near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

요한복음 6:24.content: When the multitude therefore saw that Jesus wasn’t there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats, and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.

요한복음 6:25.content: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they asked him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”

요한복음 6:26.content: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, you seek me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves, and were filled.

요한복음 6:27.content: Don’t work for the food which perishes, but for the food which remains to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For God the Father has sealed him.”

요한복음 6:28.content: They said therefore to him, “What must we do, that we may work the works of God?”

요한복음 6:29.content: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”

요한복음 6:30.content: They said therefore to him, “What then do you do for a sign, that we may see and believe you? What work do you do?

요한복음 6:31.content: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness. As it is written, ‘He gave them bread out of heaven to eat.’ ”

요한복음 6:32.content: Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, it wasn’t Moses who gave you the bread out of heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread out of heaven.

요한복음 6:33.content: For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world.”

요한복음 6:34.content: They said therefore to him, “Lord, always give us this bread.”

요한복음 6:35.content: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will not be hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.

요한복음 6:36.content: But I told you that you have seen me, and yet you don’t believe.

요한복음 6:37.content: All those whom the Father gives me will come to me. He who comes to me I will in no way throw out.

요한복음 6:38.content: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.

요한복음 6:39.content: This is the will of my Father who sent me, that of all he has given to me I should lose nothing, but should raise him up at the last day.

요한복음 6:40.content: This is the will of the one who sent me, that everyone who sees the Son, and believes in him, should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”

요한복음 6:41.content: The Jews therefore murmured concerning him, because he said, “I am the bread which came down out of heaven.”

요한복음 6:42.content: They said, “Isn’t this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then does he say, ‘I have come down out of heaven?’ ”

요한복음 6:43.content: Therefore Jesus answered them, “Don’t murmur among yourselves.

요한복음 6:44.content: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up in the last day.

요한복음 6:45.content: It is written in the prophets, ‘They will all be taught by God.’ Therefore everyone who hears from the Father and has learned, comes to me.

요한복음 6:46.content: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except he who is from God. He has seen the Father.

요한복음 6:47.content: Most certainly, I tell you, he who believes in me has eternal life.

요한복음 6:48.content: I am the bread of life.

요한복음 6:49.content: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness and they died.

요한복음 6:50.content: This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, that anyone may eat of it and not die.

요한복음 6:51.content: I am the living bread which came down out of heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. Yes, the bread which I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”

요한복음 6:52.content: The Jews therefore contended with one another, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”

요한복음 6:53.content: Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you don’t have life in yourselves.

요한복음 6:54.content: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.

요한복음 6:55.content: For my flesh is food indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.

요한복음 6:56.content: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood lives in me, and I in him.

요한복음 6:57.content: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father; so he who feeds on me, he will also live because of me.

요한복음 6:58.content: This is the bread which came down out of heaven—not as our fathers ate the manna, and died. He who eats this bread will live forever.”

요한복음 6:59.content: He said these things in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.

요한복음 6:60.content: Therefore many of his disciples, when they heard this, said, “This is a hard saying! Who can listen to it?”

요한복음 6:61.content: But Jesus knowing in himself that his disciples murmured at this, said to them, “Does this cause you to stumble?

요한복음 6:62.content: Then what if you would see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?

요한복음 6:63.content: It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and are life.

요한복음 6:64.content: But there are some of you who don’t believe.” For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who didn’t believe, and who it was who would betray him.

요한복음 6:65.content: He said, “For this cause I have said to you that no one can come to me, unless it is given to him by my Father.”

요한복음 6:66.content: At this, many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.

요한복음 6:67.content: Jesus said therefore to the twelve, “You don’t also want to go away, do you?”

요한복음 6:68.content: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.

요한복음 6:69.content: We have come to believe and know that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

요한복음 6:70.content: Jesus answered them, “Didn’t I choose you, the twelve, and one of you is a devil?”

요한복음 6:71.content: Now he spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray him, being one of the twelve.
